analysis_description: Merged **origin/main** for `Prosperity4Data/ROUND_4/` and `round4work/`. Round 4 Phase 1 tape study: 4,281 trade rows matched to price grid (days 1–3); horizons K∈{5,20,100} in **timestamp-bar steps**; aggressor inferred vs BBO; stratification by spread tertile bins and session tertile on clock timestamp.
note: `analyze_phase1.py` also writes `outputs/trades_enriched.csv` when run; it is gitignored (large).
used_to_decide: Phase 1 gate object appended as next array element. Baseline trader_v0 documents Round 4 sim harness for joint-gate extract leg before Mark-conditioned execution.

ops_ping_alignment: Phase 1 bullets covered by `analyze_phase1.py` + outputs (see `outputs/PHASE1_PING_CHECKLIST.md`). **K** = price-bar / unique-timestamp steps (documented in `PHASE1_SUMMARY.md`). **7** distinct participant strings on tape: counts in `outputs/phase1_participant_print_counts.csv`.
phase1_post_hoc_tape_refinement: Later scripts (**phase8+**) add **Sonic joint gate × (buyer,seller) × hydro** tables; the original Phase-1 **participant null** for generic Mark×symbol markouts is **not** replaced—gate-conditioned hydro is a **separate, stronger** tape signal.

bullet_evidence.conclusion: No (Mark, aggressor side, symbol, K) cell reached **|Welch t vs spread-matched pool| ≥ 2** with **n≥40**; max |t| with n≥20 was **~0.65** (Mark 55 aggr_sell extract). **Null** for informed-style same-asset markout at these horizons on three days.

bullet_evidence.bullet: 2) Deviation from bot baseline (cell mean fwd_same_20 by buyer,seller,symbol,spread_bin)
bullet_evidence.conclusion: Residual means are **~0** at scale of rounding; dominant structure is explained by the cell means (**no** large stable second-order anomaly in fwd_same_20).

**Mark 01→Mark 22** dominates counts (basket seller), **Mark 14↔Mark 38** on hydro notional; tape-order 2-step motifs are mostly **within-pair churn**. Lagged signed-flow vs extract fwd20 correlations are **small |r|** (no strong Granger-style lead in this coarse test).

Prints in **same (day,timestamp)** bursts (≥4 rows) show **higher mean fwd_EXTRACT_20** than isolated prints (**0.345 vs 0.095**) but Welch **t≈1.70** (p≈0.09 one-sided) — **suggestive only**, not a tight statistical edge across days alone.
Party-conditioned mean **fwd_same_20** on extract prints is near **0** for all Marks in this proxy; **no** clear “who systematically picks off mid” ranking beyond noise at K=20.

top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.name: Burst-time extract drift (weak)
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.rule_sketch: After same-timestamp multi-symbol burst (≤4 prints), extract mid **+20 bars** tends slightly up vs control.
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.effect: mean fwd_EXTRACT_20 ≈ +0.25 higher in burst bucket vs non-burst; Welch t≈1.7
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.n: 1395 burst vs 2884 non-burst prints with valid fwd
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.caveat: Not significant at α=0.05 two-sided; overlapping forward windows; not validated out-of-sample (only 3 days).

top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.name: Structural flow: Mark 01 → Mark 22
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.rule_sketch: Treat as **schedule / inventory** prior for MM (expect voucher sell pressure from 22 when 01 buys), not as a standalone mid-alpha.
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.caveat: Does not by itself predict signed fwd mid in our tests.

top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.name: Hydro duopoly Mark 14 ↔ Mark 38 × joint gate → forward extract (refined)
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.rule_sketch: **phase8+**: conditional on **Sonic joint tight** vs loose, **HYDRO** 14→38 / 38→14 shows **higher mean fwd_EXTRACT_20** (pooled); **phase11** shows single aggressor type per direction; **phase12** LODO: 14→38 stable on 2-day trains.
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.effect: Pooled δ(tight−loose) ≈ **+1.6** (14→38) and **+1.0** (38→14) on **fwd_EXTRACT_20**; K=5/20/100 in `phase9_*`
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.n: 496+507 prints with gate merge (varies by K valid)
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.caveat: Supersedes the pre-gate **rank-3** hydro line; see **phase8+** for gate-conditioned markouts. **Sim** (v8/v9/v12) does not yet translate this into **VEV** **`worse`** PnL.

top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.name: Mark 55 on extract (balanced MM)
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.rule_sketch: High touch rate on extract both sides; candidate for **neutral** reference bot when modeling microstructure later.
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.effect: ~43% buy/sell share on extract in r4_initial_analysis_summary (qual cross-check)

top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.name: Joint-gate extract MM (execution thesis)
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.rule_sketch: Sonic gate + extract-only quoting (trader_v0) as sim baseline **before** layering Mark filters in Phase 2.
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.effect: worse total PnL **3184** vs all **6012** on days 1–3 (not mid markout)
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.caveat: Uses R3-style gate on R4 tape; does not use counterparty fields yet.

no_edge_findings: Aggressor-tagged **participant × symbol × K** markouts: **no** |t|≥2 with n≥40 vs spread-matched pool.
Baseline-adjusted fwd_same_20 residuals show **no** persistent pair-level alpha.
Passive-party extract fwd20 proxy: **flat** across Marks.
next_phase: Phase 2 may condition the burst hint and pair structure on **finer windows**, **microprice**, and **Mark×burst** interactions; Phase 3 will re-test inside Sonic gate on R4.

analysis_description: Round 4 **Phase 2** tape + sim: added ``analyze_phase2.py`` (named-pair × burst proximity ±500, VEV_5300 MR vs trend near 01→22 bursts, leave-one-day-out burst Welch, microprice spread vs short vol on 5300, **bar-grid** signed-flow lead–lag, **book** tight5300 × burst on **all** prints’ extract fwd20, smile-lite cross-VEV rolling std by pair, Mark01→22 passive-seller fwd, Mark22 rolling pressure vs extract). ``trader_v1`` tests extract cash-leg spread cap from microstructure.
used_to_decide: See ``round4_phase2_complete`` next object for ranked edges vs Phase 1 and sim tie on v1.

interaction_with_phase1.burst_extract_effect: **Refines and partly falsifies** Phase 1 pooled burst hint: leave-one-day-out Welch shows **day 1 holdout reverses** sign (burst **worse** than non-burst on that day); days 2–3 still positive. Phase 1 t≈1.7 was **over-optimistic** for cross-day stability.
interaction_with_phase1.participant_null: **Confirmed** — Phase 2 pair×burst buckets still show no clean universal directional markout (e.g. Mark01→22 on VEV_5300 slightly negative mean fwd20 near bursts).
interaction_with_phase1.microprice_sim: Weak spread→short-vol corr on 5300 bars (~0.09). **Cash-leg spread cap** in sim (**trader_v1**) does not beat v0 unless cap is **so tight (4)** that the trader never quotes (**0** PnL); caps 6/8/12 **tie v0** at 3184 worse.

top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.name: Book tight 5300 × burst → higher extract fwd20 (tape, all prints)
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.effect: tight5300 & burst: mean fwd_EXTRACT_20 ≈ **0.338** (n=1389) vs tight & not burst **0.080** (n=2661)
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.caveat: Not same as Sonic joint gate on every bar; overlapping fwd windows; day-1 LODO weakens burst-only trading.

top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.name: Mark 55 → Mark 01 on extract near 01→22 activity (adverse proxy)
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.effect: Near-burst bucket: mean fwd20 on extract prints **≈ -1.7** (n=30) vs far bucket **+0.25** (n=214)
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.caveat: Small n; execution rule would be **avoid quoting** into that pair context, not a directional bet.

top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.name: Burst extract drift (conditional, unstable by day)
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.effect: Pooled Welch t≈1.7 (Phase 1); holdout day **1** t≈**-1.17** (burst mean below non-burst)
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.caveat: Treat as **hypothesis for more days**, not production signal on 3 days alone.

top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.name: VEV_5300 spread vs next-5-bar |Δmid| (microstructure)
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.caveat: Too weak alone; may combine with gate in Phase 3.

top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.name: Sim: extract BBO width filter (Phase 2 execution test)
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.effect: Grid 6/8/12: **no PnL change** vs v0 on R4 worse; **4**: zero participation
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.caveat: Shows cash-leg filter **not** a free improvement here; need counterparty or burst **observable** in-state for Phase 3.

phase2_unproven_or_low_power: Same-timestamp cross-flow merge is **too sparse** for extract↔5300; replaced by **bar-grid** flow file.
Bar-grid flow↔dmid correlations are **near zero** at most lags (see phase2_cross_leadlag_bar_grid_flow.csv).
Smile-lite tension by pair is descriptive only (phase2_smile_lite_tension_by_pair.csv).

analysis_description: Round 4 **Phase 3**: re-read `round3work/vouchers_final_strategy/STRATEGY.txt` and `ORIGINAL_DISCORD_QUOTES.txt` (inclineGod spread focus; Sonic joint gate). Implemented ``analyze_phase3.py`` — R4 **inner-join** 5200+5300+extract on ``timestamp``, ``tight`` = both spreads≤2, K=20 fwd on extract mid (same logic as ``analyze_vev_5200_5300_tight_gate_r3.py``). inclineGod outputs: correlation matrices + ``phase3_r4_inclineGod_panels.png``. Merged gate onto enriched trades: **gate×burst** extract fwd20, **Mark pair × gate** markouts (head sample CSV). Reference R3 paths in ping used ``previous round work/``; this repo uses ``round3work/vouchers_final_strategy/``.
used_to_decide: Append ``round4_phase3_complete``; sim ``trader_v2`` tests one Mark×gate interaction from tape.

comparison_to_phase1_2_without_joint_gat.mid_forward_extract: Phase 1 burst-only lift on extract fwd20 was **modest** (Welch t≈1.7 pooled) and **unstable by day** (Phase 2 LODO). **Joint Sonic gate** on R4 **price bars** yields **much larger** tight vs not mean fwd and **p≈10⁻⁵–10⁻¹¹** all three days (`phase3_joint_gate_summary_r4.txt`) — **strongly supports** Sonic’s “cleaner signal when both legs tight” for **mid** dynamics.
comparison_to_phase1_2_without_joint_gat.burst_under_gate: `phase3_gate_x_burst_extract_fwd20.csv`: under **joint tight**, **non-burst** timestamps show **higher** mean fwd extract (0.431, n=769) than **burst** (0.338, n=1389). Off-gate burst rows are **rare** (n=6). The gate **re-orders** burst vs quiet relative to Phase-1 burst-only pooling.
comparison_to_phase1_2_without_joint_gat.counterparty_x_gate: `phase3_mark_pair_symbol_gate_markout.csv` shows **interaction** (e.g. Mark14→38 hydro: worse fwd20 in **tight** than **loose**). **Sim falsification:** `trader_v2` (skip when Mark55→Mark01 hits extract tape) **lowers** `worse` PnL **3184→2494** — tape-conditional skip is **not** a free execution edge here.

top_5_tierA_or_ranked_findings.name: Joint gate: extract mid forward (K=20) tight vs loose
inclineGod spread–spread (R4 pooled bars)
top_5_tierA_or_ranked_findings.effect: corr(s5200,s5300)≈**0.18–0.27** per day summary; voucher spreads vs extract spread near **0** in pooled corr matrix

top_5_tierA_or_ranked_findings.name: Joint tight × burst on extract fwd20 (all trades)
Mark pair × gate markouts (selected pairs)
top_5_tierA_or_ranked_findings.effect: Structured differences by gate for same (buyer,seller,symbol) cells

top_5_tierA_or_ranked_findings.name: Sim: Mark55→Mark01 skip on extract aggression (`trader_v2`)

analysis_description: **Post-Phase 3** iteration: translate Phase 3 **strong joint-gate forward extract mid** finding into a **simple execution knob** — scale quoted **EXTRACT_HALF** when the Sonic gate is on. Grids on Round 4 `worse`: **size** mult 1.0–1.5 unchanged at **3184** (rounded clip 16→18); **half** mult 0.95/−216, 1.0/3184, **1.05/3382**, 1.1/530. `all` mode also rises slightly vs v0 (6036 vs 6012). Artifact: ``outputs/post_phase3_extract_half_mult_grid_worse.txt``.
used_to_decide: Ship **trader_v3** with **EXTRACT_HALF_MULT_TIGHT=1.05** as best `worse` in this grid; 1.1 breaks day 1–2 under adverse matching. Next: combine with **VEV** legs or counterparty filters only with tape-backed sim uplift.

analysis_description: **Counterparty × joint gate (tape):** ``analyze_r4_01_22_under_joint_gate.py`` — on R4 days 1–3, **every** Mark01→Mark22 print on VEV_5200/5300/5400 sits on a **joint-tight** timestamp (inner-join gate; loose count 0). Mean **fwd_same_20** on those prints: 5200 **+0.86** (n=11), 5300 **−0.12** (n=132), 5400 **+0.04** (n=263). **Sim:** ``trader_v4`` = **v3** extract + quote **5200/5300** when tight (Sonic hedge surface). **`worse`** PnL **ties v3** (3382; VEV lines 0). **`all`** total **9427** vs v3 **6036** — large voucher PnL but **not** the primary objective; day 3 VEV legs **negative** in `all` while extract carries.
used_to_decide: Keep **v3** as best `worse` extract-only; v4 documents hedge-surface MM for `all` exploration only unless VEV quoting is retuned for `worse`.

analysis_description: **Tape:** ``analyze_r4_01_22_vev_spreads_at_prints.py`` — at Mark01→Mark22 print timestamps, mean **s5200** and **s5300** from the aligned panel (e.g. day-1 VEV_5300 prints: mean s5300 **2.0**, n=32). **Sim:** ``trader_v5`` adds **EXTRA_VEV_HALF** to gate-leg VEV quotes. **Result:** exhaustive `worse` grid (extra half −0.5..+2, size 8..40) **never moves** total off **3382** (VEV **0** under `worse`). With **EXTRA=0.5**, **`all`** collapses to **6036** (no VEV PnL) vs **v4** **9427** — widening VEV quotes **removed** favorable `all` fills. **Conclusion:** for this backtester, **`worse`** is insensitive to this VEV half knob; **`all`** hedge from **v4** needs **EXTRA=0** (or different quote logic, not half-only).
used_to_decide: Do **not** ship wider gate-leg halves for `all` if hedge PnL matters; keep **v4** for `all` exploration, **v3** for `worse` until a different VEV execution model is found.

analysis_description: **Tape:** ``analyze_r4_joint_gate_m01_m22_flow.py`` — under joint tight, correlation of Mark01→Mark22 **VEV_5300** signed qty with **fwd_EXTRACT_20** is **near zero** (and slightly negative on day 1); output ``phase6_m01_m22_flow_summary.txt``. **Sim:** ``trader_v6`` = **v3** + quote **5300** only (drop **5200**). **`worse`** **3382** with **VEV_5300** line **0** (same as **v4** — adverse matching never credits voucher legs in this harness). **`all`** **8296** vs **v4** **9427** (lost **5200** `all` PnL). **Conclusion:** dropping **5200** does not help **`worse`**; **M01→M22** flow on **5300** is not a usable extract directional overlay.
used_to_decide: Keep **v3**/**v4** framing for **`worse`** vs **`all`**; **v6** documents **5300**-only hedge surface without **`worse`** uplift.

analysis_description: **Sim:** ``trader_v7`` = **v6** but when **VEV_5300** spread ticks **≤2**, pin quotes to **BBO** (touch); else winged half. **Hypothesis:** winged half can skip orders (**bid≥ask**) or sit inside spread; touch maximizes queue priority at the Sonic ceiling. **`worse`:** total still **3382**, **VEV_5300** **0** — **touch does not change adverse-match voucher attribution** vs **v6**. **`all`:** total **8480** vs **v6** **8296** (more **5300** on days 1–2, **−840** on day 3 **5300** vs **−715** **v6**). **Conclusion:** touch is an **`all`**-mode shape change only here; **`worse`** remains extract-only PnL for this strategy family on R4 days 1–3.
used_to_decide: Do not rely on BBO-touch **5300** for **`worse`** edge on this tape; next iterations need a different matching story (e.g. cross-product priority) or accept extract-only **`worse`**.

analysis_description: **Post-phase (Phases 1–3 complete):** **Tape** — ``analyze_r4_gate_pair_stability.py``: (buyer,seller,symbol) split by **joint tight** vs **loose**; outcome **fwd_EXTRACT_20**. Pooled: **Mark14→Mark38** and **Mark38→Mark14** on **HYDROGEL_PACK** show large tight>loose mean on **fwd_EXTRACT_20** (Welch **p≈7.5e-4** / **0.014**); **positive delta all 3 days** for both. Voucher/extract Mark pairs in the same table are **not** significant. **Sim** — ``trader_v8`` = **v3** + **HYDRO** MM when tight (**HYDRO_HALF_BASE=2.2**, size 14). **`worse`** **3685** vs **v3** **3382** (**+303** hydro; extract unchanged). **`all`** **6338** vs **v3** **6036**. Hydro PnL was **out of scope** for the original VEV+extract floor; **VEV** still **0** on **`worse`**.
used_to_decide: **Strongest counterparty×gate tape signal** in phase8 is **hydro duopoly × joint gate → fwd extract mid**; **v8** translates part of that into **positive hydro `worse`** without hurting extract. For **VEV-only** goals, **v3** remains the cleaner baseline until **`worse`** voucher fills exist.

analysis_description: **Tape (horizon + direction):** ``analyze_r4_hydro_duopoly_gate_multik.py`` — **Mark14→Mark38** **HYDROGEL_PACK**: tight>loose on **fwd_EXTRACT_K** for **K=5,20,100** (pooled Welch **p≈3.9e-4**, **7.5e-4**, **2.8e-7**); **K=100** delta **≈+4.77**. **Per-day** for **14→38**: **K=20** positive delta **all 3 days** (day2 **p≈0.036**). **Mark38→14**: pooled **K=5** **not** significant (**p≈0.41**); **K=20** and **K=100** pooled still **tight>loose** (**p≈0.014**, **0.0096**) but **day-stability is mixed** (day2 K=20 **not** sig). **Sim:** ``trader_v9`` = **v8** + **v4** VEV legs. **`worse`** **3685** (same as **v8**). **`all`** **9730** vs **v8** **6338** / **v4** **9427**.
used_to_decide: For **`all`**, **v9** is the **full** tight-regime stack (extract + hydro + **5200/5300**). For **`worse`**, **v9** adds **no** voucher PnL over **v8**; keep **v8** if hydro is allowed, else **v3** for extract-only.

analysis_description: **Execution sensitivity (hydro only):** ``trader_v10`` = **v8** with **R4_HYDRO_HALF** / **R4_HYDRO_SIZE** env overrides. ``run_v10_hydro_grid_worse.py`` grid (half **1.6–3.0**, size **8/12/16/20**, **`worse`**, **–no-progress**): **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** line stays **3382** for **all** half/size cells (day splits unchanged vs **v8**); **HYDROGEL_PACK** **`worse`** rises from **274** (half 1.6) to **333** (2.4) then **≈1296** (2.6–3.0) — **total** **3639→4709** driven **entirely** by hydro in this grid (**size** had **no** effect in the tested range). Defaults **2.2/14** match **v8**. **``trader_v10``** default backtests duplicate **v8** (**`worse`** **3685**, **`all`** **6338**).
used_to_decide: Keep **HYDRO_HALF=2.2** for **conservative** stack (**v8**/default **v10**) unless explicitly exploring **wider** hydro (higher **`worse`** total but different extract path). **v10** exists for sweeps without editing **v8**.

analysis_description: **Tape (adverse-selection slice):** ``analyze_r4_hydro_duopoly_gate_by_side.py`` — on **HYDROGEL_PACK**, **all** prints in the **Mark14→38** / **Mark38→14** cells are **one aggressor type each**: **14→38** is **only aggr_sell** (n=496 = 126T+370L); **38→14** is **only aggr_buy** (n=507). So the phase8/9 **tight>loose** lift on **fwd_EXTRACT_20** is **not** a mix of buy vs sell aggression — it is **entirely** **aggr_sell** for the first leg and **aggr_buy** for the reverse (passive counterparty is **Mark 14** in both conventions: buyer on aggr_sell prints, seller on aggr_buy prints). Pooled Welch matches the unstratified table (**14→38** **δ≈+1.61**, **p≈7.5e-4**; **38→14** **δ≈+0.999**, **p≈0.014**). **Sim:** ``trader_v11`` = **v9** + **R4_HYDRO_HALF** / **R4_HYDRO_SIZE** env (defaults **2.2/14** → same as **v9**/**v8**/**v10**). **`worse`** **3685**, **`all`** **9730**. **Spot:** **R4_HYDRO_HALF=2.6** gives **`worse`** **4678** (hydro **1296**, extract **3382**, **VEV 0**) — same hydro scaling as **v10** grid with **VEV** still zero under **`worse`**.
used_to_decide: Use **v11** when tuning **hydro** alongside **v9** VEV legs; tape shows the duopoly gate signal lives in **single-side** aggression cells, so any **fade/passive** rule should condition on **aggr_sell** vs **aggr_buy** explicitly, not a pooled “any print” story.

analysis_description: **Tape (LODO):** ``analyze_r4_hydro_duopoly_gate_lodo.py`` — **2-day** train (hold out one R4 day) **Welch** on **fwd_EXTRACT_20** tight vs loose for **HYDRO** **Mark14→38** and **Mark38→14**. **14→38:** δ **>0** and **p<0.004** for **each** holdout (train on **2+3**, **1+3**, **1+2**). **38→14:** δ **>0** all three; **p** **0.123** when day1 held out (**nT=25** train), **p≈0.012–0.023** for the other two holds. **Sim (counterparty skip):** ``trader_v12`` = **v11** but **no hydro orders** this step if **market_trades[HYDRO]** has **buyer Mark 14** and **seller Mark 38** (tape proxy for **aggr_sell** duopoly print). **Default** skip on. **`worse`** **4399** vs **v11** **3685** (**HYDRO** line **1017** vs **303**; extract **3382**; **VEV 0**). **`all`** **10444** vs **v11** **9730**. **R4_SKIP_HYDRO_14_38=0** recovers **v12**≈**v11** (**worse** **3685**). **Conclusion:** skip rule **fails** the “avoid bad hydro” hypothesis for **`worse`** on this tape; likely **increased** off-touch fills when we pull quotes only on hit timestamps.
used_to_decide: Keep **v11** for hydro+VEV stack; **do not** ship **v12**-style “skip on 14→38 print” as **improvement** under **`worse`** — re-check if matching logic is understood. LODO supports **persistence** of the 14→38 gate effect across 2-day windows except 38→14 with day1 out (**small-n** train).

analysis_description: **Phase-1 template alignment (no re-run of `analyze_phase1` required for stats):** ``analyze_r4_phase1_compliance_table.py`` writes **PHASE1_PING_CHECKLIST.md** (each Phase-1 bullet from `suggested direction.txt` → file paths + function names) and **phase1_participant_print_counts.csv** (all **7** Mark strings: buyer/seller/print counts + product list from raw trades). The existing **`round4_phase1_complete`** object was **amended in place** (same element id): added **ops_ping_alignment**, **phase1_post_hoc_tape_refinement**, and **replaced rank-3** in **top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked** with the **Sonic-gate × hydro duopoly** evidence (phase8–phase12) so the ranked list no longer claims “no extract lift” for that motif.
used_to_decide: Treat **round4work** Phase-1 ops checklist as **covered** by the mapped artifacts; new work continues under **r4_phase1_marks/outputs/phase8+** and sim **trader_v*** without reopening the Phase-2/3 gate objects.
